name: Whalesborough Cheese
specialism: On-site cheesemakers — small-batch cow and goat milk
description: Seventeen-year-old on-site cheesemaking operation — small-batch, cow and goat milk, award-winning. Smuggler is the house staple; Miss Muffet a soft cow's-milk cheese; Nanny Muffet the goats' cheese.
story_intro: The cheese is made down the lane from where you are sitting. Most of what is sold across the country starts as morning milk a five-minute walk from the cellar.

name: Hoof to Block
specialism: High-welfare grass-fed meat with nose-to-tail butchery
description: Bradworthy-based farmers and butchers running a true nose-to-tail operation. Single-farm cuts make it to The Weir within forty-eight hours.

name: Neetfield Market Garden
description: On-site market garden — left as you cross the bridge at the start of the drive. The Weir kitchen's day starts here, in the polytunnels.

name: Celtic Fish & Game
specialism: Day-boat fish, smoked fish and seasonal game
description: Family-run St Ives merchant since 1985 — what landed yesterday is what's on the plate today.

name: Trewithen Dairy
description: Family dairy that supplies the milk, yoghurt and clotted cream across the resort — from the kids' Crusha through to the granola bowl.

name: Cornish Coffee Company
description: Hand-roasted single-origin coffee — the only beans pulled at The Weir and at every spa-day welcome juice add-on.

name: Snout & Trotter
specialism: Free-range Cornish pork sausages and bacon
description: Chris Jopling's Marhamchurch business — traditional techniques, free-range pork. The sausage on the breakfast plate.

name: Phillip Warren Butchers
description: Four-generation Launceston butchers supplying the 6oz steak burger that goes by the name 'Warren's' on the lunch menu.

name: Olivers North Coast Wines
description: Bude-based wine merchant curating the wine list — small Cornish bottles alongside small-grower bottles from France, Italy, Spain and beyond.

name: Tregida Smokehouse
description: Cornish smokehouse — the source of the mackerel on the lunch jacket and the smoked salmon on the breakfast Eggs Royale.

name: Land Meets Sea
description: Outdoor grass-reared lamb and beef raised on permanent meadow pasture under a mile from the coast. Salt air adds the flavour.

name: Cornish Orchards
specialism: Cornish ciders and sparkling soft drinks
description: Liskeard-based orchard producing the cider and elderflower on the soft drinks list.

name: Whalesborough Honey
description: Honey from the estate's 15,000-strong Cornish Black Bee colony — used in the granola and available on the breakfast toast plate.

name: Marhamchurch Bakery
description: Local Marhamchurch bakery — the cream-tea scones and the daily granary loaf.
